name: Housing Authority Of Yamhill County
description_short: Non-profit housing authority providing affordable rental housing, homeownership assistance, and home improvement programs for low-income Yamhill County residents.
description_long:
The Housing Authority of Yamhill County (HAYC) operates as a non-profit organization dedicated to providing affordable housing solutions across multiple program types. Established to serve Yamhill County residents, HAYC operates alongside the Yamhill County Affordable Housing Corporation (YCAHC) and the Yamhill Housing Resource Center as integrated entities sharing a mission that housing extends beyond shelter to include community support and self-sufficiency.

HAYC offers a comprehensive suite of housing programs including family rental housing, farm labor housing, senior housing, and special needs housing. The organization also provides homeownership pathways through its home improvement and foreclosure prevention programs, alongside a resource center offering financial counseling and educational support. Their flagship upcoming development, Stratus Village, represents a 175-unit affordable apartment complex opening winter 2025 with units targeted at households earning 30-60% of Area Median Income (AMI), with waiting list registration opening spring 2025.

What distinguishes HAYC is its integrated approach combining direct property ownership and management with comprehensive support services. Rather than operating solely as a housing voucher administrator, HAYC develops and maintains its own affordable housing stock throughout Yamhill County while offering Family Self-Sufficiency programs designed to help residents achieve economic independence. The organization actively partners with contractors and employers, indicating a community-embedded development model.

HAYC is appropriate for low-income families and individuals seeking stable, affordable housing in Yamhill County. However, the organization does not provide credit repair, emergency cash loans, or debt relief services—it focuses exclusively on housing assistance and related support services. Prospective residents should expect income verification requirements and potentially lengthy waiting lists, particularly for the new Stratus Village development.
pros: Owns and operates affordable rental properties throughout Yamhill County, providing direct housing access rather than vouchers alone
Offers multiple housing types including family, farm labor, senior, and special needs housing to serve diverse populations
Developing 175-unit Stratus Village complex with affordable units at 30-60% AMI targeting working families and seniors
Provides integrated Family Self-Sufficiency programs beyond housing to support economic independence
Free resource center and counseling services available to both current participants and community members
Transparent income limit guidelines and application process published on website
Active contractor and employer partnerships supporting local economic development
cons: Limited geographic service area restricted to Yamhill County only
Waiting lists likely for popular programs, particularly new Stratus Village development opening 2025
No emergency or short-term housing solutions for immediate crisis situations
Income restrictions mean many middle-income households will not qualify for assistance
Website lacks detailed information about application timelines, approval rates, or current wait times
best_for: Low-income families seeking stable, affordable rental housing in Yamhill County
Seniors and individuals with special housing needs within the service area
Farm laborers and agricultural workers needing specialized housing
Homeowners seeking foreclosure prevention counseling or home improvement assistance
diagnosis: Best for low-income Yamhill County residents seeking stable, affordable housing with integrated support services. Main caveat: this is a housing provider, not a credit or financial assistance company—it addresses housing stability but cannot help with debt, credit repair, or emergency cash needs.
services: Family housing rentals at affordable rates
Family Self-Sufficiency program with counseling and support
Housing resource center with educational materials
Stratus Village affordable apartment complex (opening winter 2025)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
